verses.sanskrit:
ईश्वरानुग्रहादेव पुंसामद्वैतवासना ।
महद्भयपरित्राणाद्विप्राणामुपजायते ।।१।।
verses.english: The desire to gain the knowledge of non-duality arises in humans by the grace of the universal mind, which relieves them of greatest fears.

verses.sanskrit:
येनेदं पूरितं सर्वमात्मनैवाअत्मनात्मनि ।
निराकारं कथं वन्दे ह्यभिन्नं शिवमव्ययम् ।।२।।
verses.english: How can I greet that Consciousness which is formless, complete, undivided, pervades everything and all beings?

verses.sanskrit:
पञ्चभूतात्मकं विश्वं मरीचिजलसन्निभम् ।
कस्याप्यहो नमस्कुर्यामहमेको निरञ्जनः ।। ३।।
verses.english: The physical world is a mirage. I alone exist as most pure. Whom should I bow to?

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मैव केवलं सर्वं भेदाभेदो न विद्यते ।
अस्ति नास्ति कथं ब्रूयां विस्मयः प्रतिभाति मे ।। ४।।
verses.english: Consciousness is everything, differences or similarities in it cannot be known. I am filled with wonder, how to say that it exists or does not exist?

verses.sanskrit:
वेदान्तसारसर्वस्वं ज्ञानं विज्ञानमेव च ।
अहमात्मा निराकारः सर्वव्यापी स्वभावतः ।। ५।।
verses.english: The essence of Vedanta philosophy, all knowledge and sciences is that the nature of the Consciousness is omnipresence and formlessness.

verses.sanskrit:
यो वै सर्वात्मको देवो निष्कलो गगनोपमः ।
स्वभावनिर्मलः शुद्धः स एवायं न संशयः ।। ६।।
verses.english: There is no doubt that the universal Consciousness is by nature very pure, taintless, effulgent, and one, just like clear sky.

verses.sanskrit:
अहमेवाव्ययोऽनन्तः शुद्धविज्ञानविग्रहः ।
सुखं दुःखं न जानामि कथं कस्यापि वर्तते ।। ७।।
verses.english: I, Consciousness, am pure, indestructible, infinite, I am pure knowledge itself. Do not know joy or sorrow, what to say about them?

verses.sanskrit:
न मानसं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे
न कायिकं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे ।
न वाचिकं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे
ज्ञानामृतं शुद्धमतीन्द्रियोऽहम् ।।८।।
verses.english: I have no mental actions, neither good nor bad. No bodily and verbal actions too, neither good nor bad. I am the essence of knowledge and am beyond the senses. I am so pure.

verses.sanskrit:
मनो वै गगनाकारं मनो वै सर्वतोमुखम् ।
मनोऽतीतं मनः सर्वं न मनः परमार्थतः ।।९।।
verses.english: Mind is vast. Mind is multifaceted. Mind is time or past memories. Everything that is manifested is Mind. However, if seen from highest perspective, there is no Mind.

verses.sanskrit:
अहमेकमिदं सर्वं व्योमातीतं निरन्तरम् ।
पश्यामि कथमात्मानं प्रत्यक्षं वा तिरोहितम् ।। १०।।
verses.english: I am the only one that exists. I am beyond space, unbroken and continuous. How can the Consciousness be perceived directly or indirectly?

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमेवमेकं हि कथं न बुध्यसे
समं हि सर्वेषु विमृष्टमव्ययम् ।
सदोदितोऽसि त्वमखण्डितः प्रभो
दिवा च नक्तं च कथं हि मन्यसे ।। ११।।
verses.english: Why don’t you understand that you, the Consciousness, are one. It is the same one in everyone. It is ever present, ever shining. How can you say it varies like day and night?

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मानं सततं विद्धि सर्वत्रैकं निरन्तरम् ।
अहं ध्याता परं ध्येयमखण्डं खण्ड्यते कथम् ।। १२।।
verses.english: Know Consciousness as continuous, one everywhere, unbroken and changeless. How can you possibly divide yourself, the indivisible Consciousness into a meditator and an object of meditation?

verses.sanskrit:
न जातो न मृतोऽसि त्वं न ते देहः कदाचन ।
सर्वं ब्रह्मेति विख्यातं ब्रवीति बहुधा श्रुतिः ।। १३।।
verses.english: You were never a body, you do not die nor were born. The famous scriptures often say – Everything is “All that is”.

verses.sanskrit:
स बाह्याभ्यन्तरोऽसि त्वं शिवः सर्वत्र सर्वदा ।
इतस्ततः कथं भ्रान्तः प्रधावसि पिशाचवत् ।। १४।।
verses.english: Internally or externally, you are just one Consciousness, everywhere and all the time. Why are you running around in delusion here and there like a ghost?

verses.sanskrit:
संयोगश्च वियोगश्च वर्तते न च ते न मे ।
न त्वं नाहं जगन्नेदं सर्वमात्मैव केवलम् ।। १५।।
verses.english: Union or divisions do not exist in me or you. Everything is Consciousness alone, there is no separate you, me or the world.

verses.sanskrit:
शब्दादिपञ्चकस्यास्य लैवासि त्वं न ते पुनः।
त्वमेव परमं तत्त्वमतः किं परितप्यसे ।। १५।।
verses.english: You are not the object of five senses, such as sounds etc. You, the Consciousness, is the primordial substance, the only reality.  Why do you suffer at all?

verses.sanskrit:
जन्म मृत्युर्न ते चित्तं बन्धमोक्षौ शुभाशुभौ ।
कथं रोदिषि रे वत्स नामरूपं न ते न मे ।। १७।।
verses.english: There is no birth or death for you, and you are not bounded or liberated. You are not a mind. You are not good or evil. Why do you cry, my son? Both you and I are not forms or names.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो चित्त कथं भ्रान्तः प्रधावसि पिशाचवत् ।
अभिन्नं पश्य चात्मानं रागत्यागात्सुखी भव ।। १८।।
verses.english: Mind, why are you running around in delusion, like a ghost? See that you are not different from the Consciousness, give up attachments and be happy.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमेव तत्त्वं हि विकारवर्जितं
निष्कम्पमेकं हि विमोक्षविग्रहम् ।
न ते च रागो ह्यथवा विरागः
कथं हि सन्तप्यसि कामकामतः ।। १९।।
verses.english: Your reality or substance is not modifiable. It cannot be shaken, joined or disintegrated. You never had any attachments or aversions. Why are you suffering because of all these random desires?

verses.sanskrit:
वदन्ति श्रुतयः सर्वाः निर्गुणं शुद्धमव्ययम् ।
अशरीरं समं तत्त्वं तन्मां विद्धि न संशयः ।। २०।।
verses.english: Like all those scriptures say, Consciousness is very pure, devoid of any qualities or imperfections. It has no body or form, it is the only Truth. There is no doubt that I am that.

verses.sanskrit:
साकारमनृतं विद्धि निराकारं निरन्तरम् ।
एतत्तत्त्वोपदेशेन न पुनर्भवसम्भवः ।। २१।।
verses.english: Know that everything that has a form is virtual, an illusion. The formless one is eternal and true. Once you understand this teaching, reincarnation will be impossible.

verses.sanskrit:
एकमेव समं तत्त्वं वदन्ति हि विपश्चितः ।
रागत्यागात्पुनश्चित्तमेकानेकं न विद्यते ।। २२।।
verses.english: Seers say that truth is oneness. Mind, when detached, does not see the divisions in oneness anymore.

verses.sanskrit:
अनात्मरूपं च कथं समाधि-
रात्मस्वरूपं च कथं समाधिः ।
अस्तीति नास्तीति कथं समाधि-
र्मोक्षस्वरूपं यदि सर्वमेकम् ।। २३।।
verses.english: How can something that is not Consciousness be in Samadhi? How can something that is already Consciousness be in Samadhi? How can something that is ever one and has ultimate freedom, exists as well as not exist, be in Samadhi?

verses.sanskrit:
विशुद्धोऽसि समं तत्त्वं विदेहस्त्वमजोऽव्ययः ।
जानामीह न जानामीत्यात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। २४।।
verses.english: I am disembodied, formless, changeless pure Truth. How can you say I know the Consciousness or I don’t know it?

verses.sanskrit:
तत्त्वमस्यादिवाक्येन स्वात्मा हि प्रतिपादितः ।
नेति नेति श्रुतिर्ब्रूयादनृतं पाञ्चभौतिकम् ।। २५।।
verses.english: As the scriptures say, “I am that” and such sentences establish the truth of the Consciousness. As they say – it is not composed of the elements, it is “not this” “not that”.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मन्येवात्मना सर्वं त्वया पूर्णं निरन्तरम् ।
ध्याता ध्यानं न ते चित्तं निर्लज्जं ध्यायते कथम् ।। २६।।
verses.english: You are Consciousness and everything is Consciousness, which is complete and unbroken without boundaries. You are not the meditator nor the object of meditation. Why does this shameless mind keep meditating?

verses.sanskrit:
शिवं न जानामि कथं वदामि
शिवं न जानामि कथं भजामि ।
अहं शिवश्चेत्परमार्थथतत्त्वं
समस्वरूपं गगनोपमं च ।। २७।।
verses.english: I do not know what is Consciousness, how can I even talk about it or worship it? I am Consciousness, like sky or empty space. That is the ultimate Truth.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं तत्त्वं समं तत्त्वं कल्पनाहेतुवर्जितम् ।
ग्राह्यग्राहकनिर्मुक्तं स्वसंवेद्यं कथं भवेत् ।। २८।।
verses.english: I am not like a truth that can be imagined. How can I know myself via a relation like giver and given?

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्तरूपं न हि वस्तु किंचि-
त्तत्त्वस्वरूपं न हि वस्तु किंचित् ।
आत्मैकरूपं परमार्थतत्त्वं
न हिंसको वापि न चाप्यहिंसा ।। २९।।
verses.english: No object is infinite, no object has any reality. Only Consciousness is the ultimate Truth. It cannot be destroyed nor can it destroy.

verses.sanskrit:
विशुद्धोऽसि समं तत्त्वं विदेहमजमव्ययम् ।
विभ्रमं कथमात्मार्थे विभ्रान्तोऽहं कथं पुनः ।। ३०।।
verses.english: I am pure, formless, unborn, imperishable and the only Truth. How can there be any confusion about this Consciousness? How can I get confused ever again after knowing this?

verses.sanskrit:
घटे भिन्ने घटाकाशं सुलीनं भेदवर्जितम् ।
शिवेन मनसा शुद्धो न भेदः प्रतिभाति मे ।। ३१।।
verses.english: Once a clay pot is broken, the space inside it cannot be differentiated from space surrounding it. Once the mind is purified of ignorance, I cannot see any difference between the Mind and the Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
न घटो न घटाकाशो न जीवो न जीवविग्रहः ।
केवलं ब्रह्म संविद्धि वेद्यवेदकवर्जितम् ।। ३२।।
verses.english: Ultimately, there is no clay pot nor is there any space in the pot. Similarly, there are no disembodied or embodied minds. Know that there is just “All that is”, with no division of the experience and the experiencer.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वत्र सर्वदा सर्वमात्मानं सततं ध्रुवम् ।
सर्वं शून्यमशून्यं च तन्मां विद्धि न संशयः ।। ३३।।
verses.english: Consciousness is everything, it is everywhere eternally present. It is something and nothing at once, know this, have no doubts about it.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदा न लोका न सुरा न यज्ञा
वर्णाश्रमो नैव कुलं न जातिः ।
न धूममार्गो न च दीप्तिमार्गो
ब्रह्मैकरूपं परमार्थतत्त्वम् ।। ३४।।
verses.english: There are no scriptures or Vedas, no worlds, no gods, no offering ceremonies. There are no races, no lineages, no castes. There are no paths of darkness or of light. The ultimate Truth is “All that is”, oneness without form.

verses.sanskrit:
व्याप्यव्यापकनिर्मुक्तः त्वमेकः सफलं यदि ।
प्रत्यक्षं चापरोक्षं च ह्यात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ३५।।
verses.english: If you are the one, devoid of containers and contents, how can you think that the Consciousness is directly witnessed?

verses.sanskrit:
अद्वैतं केचिदिच्छन्ति द्वैतमिच्छन्ति चापरे ।
समं तत्त्वं न विन्दन्ति द्वैताद्वैतविवर्जितम् ।। ३६।।
verses.english: Some favour non-dualism, others favour dualism. They do not know that the ultimate Truth is beyond dualism or non-dualism.

verses.sanskrit:
श्वेतादिवर्णरहितं शब्दादिगुणवर्जितम् ।
कथयन्ति कथं तत्त्वं मनोवाचामगोचरम् ।। ३७।।
verses.english: How can they even talk about this truth which is beyond the reach of the mind and language, which is not white or any other colour, which is devoid of sounds or any other qualia?

verses.sanskrit:
यदाऽनृतमिदं सर्वं देहादिगगनोपमम् ।
तदा हि ब्रह्म संवेत्ति न ते द्वैतपरम्परा ।। ३८।।
verses.english: When everything start appearing false, illusory, including the body, like empty space, then you know the Brahman, not through any dualistic traditions.

verses.sanskrit:
परेण सहजात्मापि ह्यभिन्नः प्रतिभाति मे ।
व्योमाकारं तथैवैकं ध्याता ध्यानं कथं भवेत् ।। ३९।।
verses.english: I see others also as myself naturally, not different, as just this Consciousness. How can the one which is like empty space become meditator and meditation?

verses.sanskrit:
यत्करोमि यदश्नामि यज्जुहोमि ददामि यत् ।
एतत्सर्वं न मे किंचिद्विशुद्धोऽहमजोऽव्ययः ।। ४०।।
verses.english: I am not at all that which acts, eats, performs rituals or donates etc. I am pure, unborn, immortal Self.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वं जगद्विद्धि निराकृतीदं
सर्वं जगद्विद्धि विकारहीनम् ।
सर्वं जगद्विद्धि विशुद्धदेह
सर्वं जगद्विद्धि शिवैकरूपम् ।। ४१।।
verses.english: Know that the whole existence is formless or empty, it is changeless, pure, even though seems to have a form and its nature is Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्त्वं त्वं न हि सन्देहः किं जानाम्यथवा पुनः ।
असंवेद्यं स्वसंवेद्यमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ४२।।
verses.english: There is no doubt that you the Consciousness is the Truth. What else can you know apart from this? How can you call that which knows itself as the unknown?

verses.sanskrit:
मायाऽमाया कथं तात छायाऽछाया न विद्यते ।
तत्त्वमेकमिदं सर्वं व्योमाकारं निरञ्जनम् ।। ४३।।
verses.english: My son, I do not see how there can be separation of one into illusion and reality, or shadow and light. It is all one Truth, untainted, undifferentiated like empty space.

verses.sanskrit:
आदिमध्यान्तमुक्तोऽहं न बद्धोऽहं कदाचन ।
स्वभावनिर्मलः शुद्ध इति मे निश्चिता मतिः ।। ४४।।
verses.english: I was free in the beginning, free now and will be free till the end, I was never bound. I am naturally very clean and pure, this is my firm understanding.

verses.sanskrit:
महदादि जगत्सर्वं न किंचित्प्रतिभाति मे ।
ब्रह्मैव केवलं सर्वं कथं वर्णाश्रमस्थितिः ।। ४५।।
verses.english: I do not ever see this whole universe, not even the first substance. Oneness is All that is. How can it be differentiated into races or castes?

verses.sanskrit:
जानामि सर्वथा सर्वमहमेको निरन्तरम् ।
निरालम्बमशून्यं च शून्यं व्योमादिपञ्चकम् ।। ४६।।
verses.english: I know that I am the only one there always, seen as everything. Space and physical elements are empty and have no foundation of their own.

verses.sanskrit:
न षण्ढो न पुमान्न स्त्री न बोधो नैव कल्पना ।
सानन्दो वा निरानन्दमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ४७।।
verses.english: Consciousness has no gender, it is not a man, woman or an eunuch. It is not an imagination or an idea in the mind. How can you believe that it has any happiness or suffering?

verses.sanskrit:
षडङ्गयोगान्न तु नैव शुद्धं
मनोविनाशान्न तु नैव शुद्धम् ।
गुरूपदेशान्न तु नैव शुद्धं
स्वयं च तत्त्वं स्वयमेव बुद्धम् ।। ४८।।
verses.english: Consciousness cannot be purified by the six limbed yoga practices. Cannot be purified by stopping or destroying one’s mind. Cannot be purified by teachings of a Guru. It is itself the Truth, it knows itself as beyond mind.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि पञ्चात्मको देहो विदेहो वर्तते न हि ।
आत्मैव केवलं सर्वं तुरीयं च त्रयं कथम् ।। ४९।।
verses.english: Minds embodied in physical matter do not exist, nor are there any disembodied minds. When there is only Consciousness as everything, how can you talk of three or four states of minds?

verses.sanskrit:
न बद्धो नैव मुक्तोऽहं न चाहं ब्रह्मणः पृथक् ।
न कर्ता न च भोक्ताहं व्याप्यव्यापकवर्जितः ।। ५०।।
verses.english: I was never bound so I cannot be liberated. I was never differentiated from the oneness. I’m not the doer of actions, I am not the enjoyer of consequences, I am devoid of anything, just empty.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा जलं जले न्यस्तं सलिलं भेदवर्जितम् ।
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं तद्वदभिन्नं प्रतिभाति मे ।। ५१।।
verses.english: Just like the water when added to water cannot be differentiated, I cannot see any difference in experiences and the experiencer.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि नाम न मुक्तोऽसि न बद्धोऽसि कदाचन ।
साकारं च निराकारमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ५२।।
verses.english: If it was never bound or liberated, how can the Consciousness be called as having a form or formless?

verses.sanskrit:
जानामि ते परं रूपं प्रत्यक्षं गगनोपमम् ।
यथा परं हि रूपं यन्मरीचिजलसन्निभम् ।। ५३।।
verses.english: Know this directly that your nature is like the metaphorical empty space. Rest is just like the illusory water that appears in a mirage.

verses.sanskrit:
न गुरुर्नोपदेशश्च न चोपाधिर्न मे क्रिया ।
विदेहं गगनं विद्धि विशुद्धोऽहं स्वभावतः ।। ५४।।
verses.english: I know that I am without form, pure, like limitless space naturally, not realized via a Guru’s teachings, or not because of any lofty titles or not through yogic kriyas.

verses.sanskrit:
विशुद्धोऽस्य शरीरोऽसि न ते चित्तं परात्परम् ।
अहं चात्मा परं तत्त्वमिति वक्तुं न लज्जसे ।। ५५।।
verses.english: Your highest essence is not the body or the mind, you are pure. You should not hesitate to say that I am the ultimate Truth, the Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
कथं रोदिषि रे चित्त ह्यात्मैवात्मात्मना भव ।
पिब वत्स कलातीतमद्वैतं परमामृतम् ।। ५६।।
verses.english: Mind, why are you suffering? Be the Self, the Consciousness. My son, drink the eternal nectar of non-duality.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव बोधो न चाबोधो न बोधाबोध एव च ।
यस्येदृशः सदा बोधः स बोधो नान्यथा भवेत् ।। ५७।।
verses.english: There exists neither knowledge nor ignorance. That which has such knowledge is the knowledge itself, never otherwise.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं न तर्को न समाधियोगो
न देशकालौ न गुरूपदेशः ।
स्वभावसंवित्तरहं च तत्त्व-
माकाशकल्पं सहजं ध्रुवं च ।। ५८।।
verses.english: Knowledge or logic, samadhi or yoga, space or time, master’s teachings, all these are not needed to be that Truth which is like sky, naturally steadily present.

verses.sanskrit:
न जातोऽहं मृतो वापि न मे कर्म शुभाशुभम् ।
विशुद्धं निर्गुणं ब्रह्म बन्धो मुक्तिः कथं मम ।। ५९।।
verses.english: I do not take births, I do not die. I do no auspicious or inauspicious acts. How can there be bondage or liberation for that which is purest and is devoid of any qualities?

verses.sanskrit:
यदि सर्वगतो देवः स्थिरः पूर्णो निरन्तरः ।
अन्तरं हि न पश्यामि स बाह्याभ्यन्तरः कथम् ।। ६०।।
verses.english: If I am omnipresent, effulgent, changeless, perfect and eternal, how can I see the difference between internal or external?

verses.sanskrit:
स्फुरत्येव जगत्कृत्स्नमखण्डितनिरन्तरम् ।
अहो मायामहामोहो द्वैताद्वैतविकल्पना ।। ६१।।
verses.english: The existence is bright, undivided and continuous. Maya, attachments, duality, non-duality are all imaginations.

verses.sanskrit:
साकारं च निराकारं नेति नेतीति सर्वदा ।
भेदाभेदविनिर्मुक्तो वर्तते केवलः शिवः ।। ६२।।
verses.english: Everything that has form is not-this, everything that is formless is not-this. Consciousness exists free from any divisions or similarities.

verses.sanskrit:
न ते च माता च पिता च बन्धुः
न ते च पत्नी न सुतश्च मित्रम् ।
न पक्षपाती न विपक्षपातः
कथं हि संतप्तिरियं हि चित्ते ।। ६३।।
verses.english: You have no mother, father, brother, wife, son or friends. You do not favour anything or reject anything. How is this suffering still present in your mind?

verses.sanskrit:
दिवा नक्तं न ते चित्तं उदयास्तमयौ न हि ।
विदेहस्य शरीरत्वं कल्पयन्ति कथं बुधाः ।। ६४।।
verses.english: Your mind does not rise or set like the sun in a day. How can an intelligent man imagine the formless being taking up bodies?

verses.sanskrit:
नाविभक्तं विभक्तं च न हि दुःखसुखादि च ।
न हि सर्वमसर्वं च विद्धि चात्मानमव्ययम् ।। ६५।।
verses.english: Know the Consciousness as having no parts, its not all things or some things, it is not undivided or divided, and it has no happiness or suffering.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं कर्ता न भोक्ता च न मे कर्म पुराऽधुना ।
न मे देहो विदेहो वा निर्ममेति ममेति किम् ।। ६६।।
verses.english: I am not the doer or enjoyer, do not have any old or new karma. I do not have a body nor am I bodiless. How can it be that “this is me” or “this is not me?

verses.sanskrit:
न मे रागादिको दोषो दुःखं देहादिकं न मे ।
आत्मानं विद्धि मामेकं विशालं गगनोपमम् ।। ६७।।
verses.english: I do not have any likes or dislikes, no imperfections, no sufferings or pains of the body. I know myself as one immensity, like that of the sky.

verses.sanskrit:
सखे मनः किं बहुजल्पितेन
सखे मनः सर्वमिदं वितर्क्यम् ।
यत्सारभूतं कथितं मया ते
त्वमेव तत्त्वं गगनोपमोऽसि ।। ६८।।
verses.english: O Mind, my friend, all this is of no consequence. O mind, my friend, all this is just arguments. I told you the essence of it all. You are the Truth, like the immense space.

verses.sanskrit:
येन केनापि भावेन यत्र कुत्र मृता अपि ।
योगिनस्तत्र लीयन्ते घटाकाशमिवाम्बरे ।। ६९।।
verses.english: However a yogi lives, where ever he dies, he is dissolved into everything like the space in a clay pot merges back into all space after it is broken.

verses.sanskrit:
तीर्थे चान्त्यजगेहे वा नष्टस्मृतिरपि त्यजन् ।
समकाले तनुं मुक्तः कैवल्यव्यापको भवेत् ।। ७०।।
verses.english: Does not matter if one leaves this world at a holy place or if one loses all memories also. He is freed immediately and merges into the ultimate existence.

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षांश्च द्विपदादिचराचरम् ।
मन्यन्ते योगिनः सर्वं मरीचिजलसन्निभम् ।। ७१।।
verses.english: A yogi sees the right conduct, prosperity, desires, liberation, men and all animated or unanimated, as merely like an illusion of water in a mirage.

verses.sanskrit:
अतीतानागतं कर्म वर्तमानं तथैव च ।
न करोमि न भुञ्जामि इति मे निश्चला मतिः ।। ७२।।
verses.english: I know certainly that I do not perform any actions, nor am I affected by their consequences, whether they are in past, present or future actions.

verses.sanskrit:
शून्यागारे समरसपूत-
स्तिष्ठन्नेकः सुखमवधूतः ।
चरति हि नग्नस्त्यक्त्वा गर्वं
विन्दति केवलमात्मनि सर्वम् ।। ७३।।
verses.english: With an empty mind like an unoccupied house, equanimous, an Avdhuta remains stable, renouncing pride, he wanders naked and knows everything as Consciousness itself.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रितयतुरीयं नहि नहि यत्र
विन्दति केवलमात्मनि तत्र ।
धर्माधर्मौ नहि नहि यत्र
बद्धो मुक्तः कथमिह तत्र ।। ७४।।
verses.english: There are no three or four states, I know there is only Consciousness. When there is no right or wrong conduct, how can there be bondage or liberation.

verses.sanskrit:
विन्दति विन्दति नहि नहि मन्त्रं
छन्दोलक्षणं नहि नहि तन्त्रम् ।
समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः
प्रलपितमेतत्परमवधूतः ।। ७५।।
verses.english: An Avdhuta knows no formulas, no verses, no techniques. He is unmoving, equanimous, and absorbed in the ultimate.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वशून्यमशून्यं च सत्यासत्यं न विद्यते ।
स्वभावभावतः प्रोक्तं शास्त्रसंवित्तिपूर्वकम् ।। ७६।।
verses.english: There exists no nothingness or something-ness, there are no truths or untruths. One speaks the scriptures out of one’s own nature.

verses.sanskrit:
बालस्य वा विषयभोगरतस्य वापि
मूर्खस्य सेवकजनस्य गृहस्थितस्य ।
एतद्गुरोः किमपि नैव न चिन्तनीयं
रत्नं कथं त्यजति कोऽप्यशुचौ प्रविष्टम् ।। १।।
verses.english: Do not think of those who are immature, hedonistic, idiots, servants, householders etc. as inferior. Who throws away a gem that has gone dirty?

verses.sanskrit:
नैवात्र काव्यगुण एव तु चिन्तनीयो
ग्राह्यः परं गुणवता खलु सार एव।
सिन्दूरचित्ररहिता भुवि रूपशून्या
पारं न किं नयति नौरिह गन्तुकामान्।। २।।
verses.english: Do not consider one’s scholarly qualities or oratory skills as important, get the essence of everything. Wouldn’t a well decorated and painted boat takes passengers across, just like a simple boat does?

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयत्नेन विना येन निश्चलेन चलाचलम्।
ग्रस्तं स्वभावतः शान्तं चैतन्यं गगनोपमम्।।३।।
verses.english: All that is moving and stationary is pervaded by naturally peaceful and unmoving Consciousness, like space, without any efforts.

verses.sanskrit:
अयत्नाच्चालयेद्यस्तु एकमेव चराचरम्।
सर्वगं तत्कथं भिन्नमद्वैतं वर्तते मम।।४।।
verses.english: The one and only that without any effort exists everywhere and goes everywhere, how can that be differentiated? It appears non-dual to me.

verses.sanskrit:
अहमेव परं यस्मात्सारासारतरं शिवम्।
गमागमविनिर्मुक्तं निर्विकल्पं निराकुलम्।।५।।
verses.english: I am the only one who is ultimate, the essence of all essences, the Consciousness. Free from transmigration, one and the only, calm and quiet.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वावयवनिर्मुक्तं तथाहं त्रिदशार्चितम्।
सम्पूर्णत्वान्न गृह्णामि विभागं त्रिदशादिकम्।।६।।
verses.english: Not having any parts or divisions, I am worshipped by the thirty. However, being one and complete, I do not discriminate the thirty etc.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रमादेन न सन्देहः किं करिष्यामि वृत्तिमान्।
उत्पद्यन्ते विलीयन्ते बुद्बुदाश्च यथा जले।।७।।
verses.english: What can one do, the activities of the mind give rise to ignorance, inertia and doubts. They appear and disappear like bubble in water.

verses.sanskrit:
महदादीनि भूतानि समाप्यैवं सदैव हि।
मृदुद्रव्येषु तीक्ष्णेषु गुडेषु कटुकेषु च।।८।।
verses.english: Consciousness pervades the first substance and all physical elements always. Soft or hard, sweet or bitter.

verses.sanskrit:
कटुत्वं चैव शैत्यत्वं मृदुत्वं च यथा जले।
प्रकृतिः पुरुषस्तद्वदभिन्नं प्रतिभाति मे।।९।।
verses.english: Just as the qualities of sweetness or bitterness, and coldness or fluidity cannot be differentiated from the water that has those, I do not see the existence and Consciousness as different.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वाख्यारहितं यद्यत्सूक्ष्मात्सूक्ष्मतरं परम्।
मनोबुद्धीन्द्रियातीतमकलङ्कं जगत्पतिम्।।१०।।
verses.english: Consciousness, the ruler of all universes, is beyond all descriptions, subtler than the subtlest, it is the ultimate, spotlessly clean, beyond the mind, intellect and senses.

verses.sanskrit:
ईदृशं सहजं यत्र अहं तत्र कथं भवेत्।
त्वमेव हि कथं तत्र कथं तत्र चराचरम्।।११।।
verses.english: If there exists such a simple and natural reality, how can I be there? How can you or all living and inert objects be there?

verses.sanskrit:
गगनोपमं तु यत्प्रोक्तं तदेव गगनोपमम्।
चैतन्यं दोषहीनं च सर्वज्ञं पूर्णमेव च।।१२।।
verses.english: That which is said to be like the metaphorical empty space is exactly that. Aware, perfect, all knowing and complete.

verses.sanskrit:
पृथिव्यां चरितं नैव मारुतेन च वाहितम्।
वारिणा पिहितं नैव तेजोमध्ये व्यवस्थितम्।।१३।।
verses.english: It does not walk on the earth, does not get carried away by the wind, does not drown in water, and is not located in the fire too.

verses.sanskrit:
आकाशं तेन संव्याप्तं न तद्व्याप्तं च केनचित्।
स बाह्याभ्यन्तरं तिष्ठत्यवच्छिन्नं निरन्तरम्।।१४।।
verses.english: Space is pervaded by it, but it is not pervaded by anything. It does not exist inside or outside of anything. It is undivided, eternal and continuous.

verses.sanskrit:
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तददृश्यत्वान्निर्गुणत्वाच्च योगिभिः।
आलम्बनादि यत्प्रोक्तं क्रमादालम्बनं भवेत्।।१५।।
verses.english: As mentioned by yogis, one should concentrate on the micro, the invisible and the attribute-less, in that order.

verses.sanskrit:
सतताऽभ्यासयुक्तस्तु निरालम्बो यदा भवेत्।
तल्लयाल्लीयते नान्तर्गुणदोषविवर्जितः।।१६।।
verses.english: After continuous practice, when one’s mind concentrates on almost nothing specific, one dissolves into the Consciousness, being devoid of all qualities.

verses.sanskrit:
विषविश्वस्य रौद्रस्य मोहमूर्च्छाप्रदस्य च।
एकमेव विनाशाय ह्यमोघं सहजामृतम्।।१७।।
verses.english: In order to destroy the poisonous effects of this violent worldly illusion, which produces unConsciousness and clinging, being Consciousness simply and naturally, is the best remedy.

verses.sanskrit:
भावगम्यं निराकारं साकारं दृष्टिगोचरम्।
भावाभावविनिर्मुक्तमन्तरालं तदुच्यते।।१८।।
verses.english: The formless can be felt in the mind, the forms can be seen with the eyes. That which is beyond form and formlessness, is called a vacancy.

verses.sanskrit:
बाह्यभावं भवेद्विश्वमन्तः प्रकृचिरुच्यते।
अन्तरादन्तरं ज्ञेयं नारिकेलफलाम्बुवत्।।१९।।
verses.english: The world exists externally to what is called Prakriti. Know that which in even inside it. Just like the water is inside the pulp of a coconut and the pulp in turn is inside the hard shell.

verses.sanskrit:
भ्रान्तिज्ञानं स्थितं बाह्यं सम्यग्ज्ञानं च मध्यगम्।
मध्यान्मध्यतरं ज्ञेयं नारिकेलफलाम्बुवत्।।२०।।
verses.english: Knowledge of external world is illusory. Knowledge of internal cause is the right one. Know what lies inside that interior, like water in the innermost cavity of a coconut.

verses.sanskrit:
पौर्णमास्यां यथा चन्द्र एक एवातिनिर्मलः।
तेन तत्सदृशं पश्येद्द्विधादृष्टिर्विपर्ययः।।२१।।
verses.english: Consciousness is one, like a full moon in the night, one and very pure. Seeing duality is an error.

verses.sanskrit:
अनेनैव प्रकारेण बुद्धिभेदो न सर्वगः।
दाता च धीरतामेति गीयते नामकोटिभिः।।२२।।
verses.english: Because of various reasons the omnipresent one is beyond intellect. Givers of this knowledge sing a million songs for it patiently.

verses.sanskrit:
गुरुप्रज्ञाप्रसादेन मूर्खो वा यदि पण्डितः।
यस्तु संबुध्यते तत्त्वं विरक्तो भवसागरात्।।२३।।
verses.english: Through the gracious gift of this knowledge by a Guru, idiots as well as pundits equally understand this Truth and become disillusioned and detached from this ocean of existence.

verses.sanskrit:
रागद्वेषविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वभूतहिते रतः।
दृढबोधश्च धीरश्च स गच्छेत्परमं पदम्।।२४।।
verses.english: One who is free from attachments and hate, who is involved in uplifting everyone, who has very certain knowledge, who has patience, he will attain the highest post.

verses.sanskrit:
घटे भिन्ने घटाकाश आकाशे लीयते यथा।
देहाभावे तथा योगी स्वरूपे परमात्मनि।।२५।।
verses.english: A yogi merges into the universal Consciousness after leaving the body, just like the space inside a clay pot merges with all space once it is broken.

verses.sanskrit:
उक्तेयं कर्मयुक्तानां मतिर्यान्तेऽपि सा गतिः।
न चोक्ता योगयुक्तानां मतिर्यान्तेऽपि सा गतिः।।२६।।
verses.english: It is said that the future of those who are engaged in worldly activities is determined by their state of mind at the time of their death. This is not said for the yogis.

verses.sanskrit:
या गतिः कर्मयुक्तानां सा च वागिन्द्रियाद्वदेत्।
योगिनां या गतिः क्वापि ह्यकथ्या भवतोर्जिता।।२७।।
verses.english: One can speak of/predict the destiny of those who are involved in worldly actions. It is not possible to do the same for yogis.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं ज्ञात्वा त्वमुं मार्गं योगिनां नैव कल्पितम्।
विकल्पवर्जनं तेषां स्वयं सिद्धिः प्रवर्तते।।२८।।
verses.english: Knowing this, one cannot imagine that yogis travel a destined path. Having no ignorance, for them, self-perfection happens on its own.

verses.sanskrit:
तीर्थे वान्त्यजगेहे वा यत्र कुत्र मृतोऽपि वा।
न योगी पश्यते गर्भं परे ब्रह्मणि लीयते।।२९।।
verses.english: The yogi, whether he dies at a holy place or anywhere else, never sees a womb again, he is dissolved in the universal Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
सहजमजमचिन्त्यं यस्तु पश्येत्स्वरूपं
घटति यदि यथेष्टं लिप्यते नैव दोषैः।
सकृदपि तदभावात्कर्म किञ्चिन्नकुर्यात्
तदपि न च विबद्धः संयमी वा तपस्वी।।३०।।
verses.english: Having seen himself as most natural and simple, unborn and beyond intellect, he never suffers from any faults due to desires. In that state he acts without acting. A self-restrained practitioner is hence, never bound by anything.

verses.sanskrit:
निरामयं निष्प्रतिमं निराकृतिं
निराश्रयं निर्वपुषं निराशिषम्।
निर्द्वन्द्वनिर्मोहमलुप्तशक्तिकं
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३१।।
verses.english: One becomes pure, beyond comparison, formless, independent, bodiless, desireless, has no conflicts, is detached, and gets everlasting powers, when he attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदो न दीक्षा न च मुण्डनक्रिया
गुरुर्न शिष्यो न च यन्त्रसम्पदः।
मुद्रादिकं चापि न यत्र भासते
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३२।।
verses.english: It is not done through Vedas, nor initiation, not by shaving your head, not via a Guru, not by being a student, not by wealth and worldly techniques, not by yogic postures, or not by wearing ashes.

verses.sanskrit:
न शाम्भवं शाक्तिकमानवं न वा
पिण्डं च रूपं च पदादिकं न वा।
आरम्भनिष्पत्तिघटादिकं च नो
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३३।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness not via Shambhavi, nor Kundalini, nor Mantras, not by worshipping forms and figures, not by worshipping feet or footprints and not by accomplishment of rituals or ceremonies.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य स्वरूपात्सचराचरं जगद्
उत्पद्यते तिष्ठति लीयतेऽपि वा।
पयोविकारादिव फेनबुद्बुदा-
स्तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३४।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing That which gives rise to the whole universe with its all living and inert forms, which stay there for a while and dissolve back just like foam and bubble arise out of water and dissolve back into it.

verses.sanskrit:
नासानिरोधो न च दृष्टिरासनं
बोधोऽप्यबोधोऽपि न यत्र भासते।
नाडीप्रचारोऽपि न यत्र किञ्चित्
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३५।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness not by restraining one’s breath, not by concentrating one’s gaze, not by refining one’s nervous system. In him there is no knowledge or ignorance.

verses.sanskrit:
नानात्वमेकत्वमुभत्वमन्यता
अणुत्वदीर्घत्वमहत्त्वशून्यता।
मानत्वमेयत्वसमत्ववर्जितं
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३६।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing that which is not many, which is not one, which has no sameness or otherness, which is not tiny or huge, which is not the first substance or nothingness, which cannot be known by any external means, which cannot be objectively measured and which has no similarity with anything else.

verses.sanskrit:
सुसंयमी वा यदि वा न संयमी
सुसंग्रही वा यदि वा न संग्रही।
निष्कर्मको वा यदि वा सकर्मक
स्तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्तम्।।३७।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by not disciplining one’s mind or being undisciplined, not by controlling one’s senses or letting go of them, not by being a doer or a non-doer.

verses.sanskrit:
मनो न बुद्धिर्न शरीरमिन्द्रियं
तन्मात्रभूतानि न भूतपञ्चकम्।
अहंकृतिश्चापि वियत्स्वरूपकं
तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।३८।।
verses.english: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing that which is not mind, not intellect, not body, not senses, not non-physical, not physical, not ego, and not even any exotic energetic form of some kind.

verses.sanskrit:
विधौ निरोधे परमात्मतां गते
न योगिनश्चेतसि भेदवर्जिते।
शौचं न वाशौचमलिङ्गभावना
सर्वं विधेयं यदि वा निषिध्यते।।३९।।
verses.english: Upon realizing the ultimate universal Consciousness, a yogi goes beyond restrictions of spiritual practices, he sees no duality or differences of any kind. He has no concern with being of a particular gender or being pure or impure. Upon knowing the ultimate Truth, nothing is prohibited for a yogi.

verses.sanskrit:
मनो वचो यत्र न शक्तमीरितुं
नूनं कथं तत्र गुरूपदेशता।
इमां कथामुक्तवतो गुरोस्तद्
युक्तस्य तत्त्वं हि समं प्रकाशते।।४०।।
verses.english: Where mind and speech is useless, Guru’s teachings say nothing. How can a Guru tell about that Truth which reveals itself?

verses.sanskrit:
गुणविगुणविभागो वर्तते नैव किञ्चित्
रतिविरतिविहीनं निर्मलं निष्प्रपञ्चम्।
गुणाविगुणविहीनं व्यापकं विश्वरूपं
कथमहमिह वन्दे व्योमरूपं शिवं वै।।१।।
verses.english: How can I worship that Consciousness which has no qualities and also is not quality-less, which has no attachments and detachments, which is pure and innocent, which is all pervading like space?

verses.sanskrit:
तादिवर्णरहितो नियतं शिवश्च
कार्यं हि कारणमिदं हि परं शिवश्च।
एवं विकल्परहितोऽहमलं शिवश्च
स्वात्मानमात्मनि सुमित्र कथं नमामि।।२।।
verses.english: My dear friend, how can I worship that Consciousness which I myself am, which is not white or any other colour, which is eternal and undifferentiated, which is both cause and effect.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मूलमूलरहितो हि सदोदितोऽहं
निर्धूमधूमरहितो हि सदोदितोऽहम्।
निर्दीपदीपरहितो हि सदोदितोऽहं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३।।
verses.english: I am ever present, never sets, have no beginning or beginning-less, I am in shadow and not in it, I shine brightly and I do not. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निष्कामकाममिह नाम कथं वदामि
निःसङ्गसङ्गमिह नाम कथं वदामि।
निःसारसाररहितं च कथं वदामि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।४।।
verses.english: How can I speak of that which is desireless and desirous, is attached and unattached, has substance and is still empty, I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
अद्वैतरूपमखिलं हि कथं वदामि
द्वैतस्वरूपमखिलं हि कथं वदामि।
नित्यं त्वनित्यमखिलं हि कथं वदामि
ज्ञानमृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।५।।
verses.english: How can I speak of that whole which is non-dual, which is expressed as dual, which manifests as both eternal and impermanent. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
स्थूलं हि नो नहि कृशं न गतागतं हि
आद्यन्तमध्यरहितं न परापरं हि।
सत्यं वदामि खलु वै परमार्थतत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।६।।
verses.english: It is neither gross nor subtle, neither goes nor comes, has no beginning, end or middle, it transcends all and does not transcend also. I speak the truth, this is the ultimate Truth, I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
संविद्धि सर्वकरणानि नभोनिभानि
संविद्धि सर्वविषयांश्च नभोनिभांश्च।
संविद्धि चैकममलं न हि बन्धमुक्तं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।७।।
verses.english: Know well that all sensory instruments are empty like space, all sense objects are empty like space, know that which is pure and boundless. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
दुर्बोधबोधगहनो न भवामि तात
दुर्लक्ष्यलक्ष्यगहनो न भवामि तात।
आसन्नरूपगहनो न भवामि तात
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।८।।
verses.english: My son, I am not beyond knowledge but I am also not some deeper knowledge which is difficult to get. I am not beyond concentrated attention, nor am I some deeper attention, I am not formless nor am I some deeper form, I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निष्कर्मकर्मदहनो ज्वलनो भवामि
निर्दुःखदुःखदहनो ज्वलनो भवामि।
निर्देहदेहदहनो ज्वलनो भवामि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।९।।
verses.english: I do not have karma, I am that fire which burns the karma, I do not have suffering, I am the fire that burns the suffering, I do not have a body, I am the fire that burns the body. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निष्पापपापदहनो हि हुताशनोऽहं
निर्धर्मधर्मदहनो हि हुताशनोऽहम्।
निर्बन्धबन्धदहनो हि हुताशनोऽहं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१०।।
verses.english: I am not the doer of wrong deeds, I am the fire that burns the wrong deeds. I am not the doer of right actions, I am the fire that burns the right actions. I am not bound, I am the fire that burns the bondage. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्भावभावरहितो न भवामि वत्स
निर्योगयोगरहितो न भवामि वत्स।
निश्चित्तचित्तरहितो न भवामि वत्स
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।११।।
verses.english: My son, I am beyond existence and non-existence, I am beyond the union and separation, I am beyond the mental activities and absence of those. I am the essence of all knowledge omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मोहमोहपदवीति न मे विकल्पो
निःशोकशोकपदवीति न मे विकल्पः।
निर्लोभलोभपदवीति न मे विकल्पो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१२।।
verses.english: One goes in attachments, but I am the unattached one, it is not my ignorance. One goes in suffering, but I am free from it, it is not my ignorance. One goes in greed, but I am not greedy, it is not my ignorance. I am the essence of all knowledge omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
संसारसन्तिलता न च मे कदाचित्
सन्तोषसन्ततिसुखो न च मे कदाचित्।
अज्ञानबन्धनमिदं न च मे कदाचित्
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१३।।
verses.english: I am never entwined in the worldly matters, contentment and pleasures resulting from them are never mine, bondage of ignorance is never mine. I am the essence of all knowledge omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
संसारसन्ततिरजो न च मे विकारः
सन्तापसन्ततितमो न च मे विकारः।
सत्त्वं स्वधर्मजनकं न च मे विकारो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१४।।
verses.english: Worldly activities do not produce any defects in me. Suffering resulting from one’s inactivity produce no defects in me. Right conduct resulting from truth produce no defects in me.

verses.sanskrit:
सन्तापदुःखजनको न विधिः कदाचित्
सन्तापयोगजनितं न कदाचित् मनः।
यस्मादहङ्कृतिरियं न च मे कदाचित
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१५।।
verses.english: I do not create the suffering or pains. A mind in union does not create suffering. Egoic activities are not me. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निष्कम्पकम्पनिधनं न विकल्पकल्पं
स्वप्नप्रबोधनिधनं न हिताहितं हि।
निःसारसारनिधनं न चराचरं हि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१६।।
verses.english: I am the end of the change and changelessness. I have no delusions or imaginations. I am the end of dream and waking state. I am neither beneficial nor harmful. I am the end of the essential and nonessential. I'm not the moving or still. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
नो वेद्यवेदकमिदं न च हेतुतर्क्यं
वाचामगोचरमिदं न मनो न बुद्धिः।
एवं कथं हि भवतः कथयामि तत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१७।।
verses.english: I'm neither the knower nor the known. I am not the cause and I'm not the effect. I am beyond speech, mind or intellect. How can the one truth be described by words? I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्भिन्नभिन्नरहितं परमार्थतत्त्व
मन्तर्बहिर्न हि कथं परमार्थतत्त्वम्।
प्राक्सम्भवं न च रतं नहि वस्तु किञ्चित्
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१८।।
verses.english: I am the ultimate truth with and without divisions having no interior and exterior, never had a beginning. I am not an object. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
रागादिदोषरहितं त्वहमेव तत्त्वं
दैवादिदोषरहितं त्वहमेव तत्त्वं।
संसारशोकरहितं त्वहमेव तत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।१९।।
verses.english: I am the ultimate truth which is free from defects of attachments, which is free from defects of destiny, which is free from defects caused by worldly existence. I am the essence of all knowledge omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
स्थानत्रयं यदि च नेति कथं तुरीयं
कालत्रयं यदि च नेति कथं दिशश्च।
शान्तं पदं हि परमं परमार्थतत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२०।।
verses.english: If I am not the three places, how can I be the forth? If I am not the three times how can I be the forth? I am the ultimate truth and extremely peaceful. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
दीर्घो लघुः पुनरितीह न मे विभागो
विस्तारसंकटमितीह न मे विभागः।
कोणं हि वर्तुलमितीह न मे विभागो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२१।।
verses.english: I do not have the divisions of big and small. I do not have the divisions of wide and narrow. I do not have the divisions of angular or circular. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
मातापितादि तनयादि न मे कदाचित्
जातं मृतं न च मनो न च मे कदाचित्।
निर्व्याकुलं स्थिरमिदं परमार्थतत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२२।।
verses.english: I never had a father, mother or children. I was never born, never died. I am not the mind. I am free of anxiety - the ultimate truth, very stable. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
शुद्धं विशुद्धमविचारमनन्तरूपं
निर्लेपलेपमविचारमनन्तरूपम्।
निष्खण्डखण्डमविचारमनन्तरूपं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२३।।
verses.english: I am infinite and unthinkable, beyond pure and impure, beyond attached and detached, beyond differentiated and undifferentiated. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मादयः सुरगणाः कथमत्र सन्ति
स्वर्गादयो वसतयः कथमत्र सन्ति।
यद्येकरूपममलं परमार्थतत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२४।।
verses.english: How can a creator, all gods and other entities be there? How can there be heavens and other worlds? I am the ultimate truth, spotlessly clean. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्नेति नेति विमलो हि कथं वदामि
निःशेषशेषविमलो हि कथं वदामि।
निर्लिङ्गलिङ्गविमलो हि कथं वदामि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२५।।
verses.english: How can I speak of that purity which is not-this and also this, which is not supported and also a foundation, which has a gender and is also genderless? I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निष्कर्मकर्मपरमं सततं करोमि
निःसङ्गसङ्गरहितं परमं विनोदम्।
निर्देहदेहरहितं सततं विनोदं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२६।।
verses.english: I am always performing actions without acting. I am the eternal bliss without attachments or detachments. I am the eternal bliss with and without a body. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
मायाप्रपञ्चरचना न च मे विकारः
कौटिल्यदम्भरचना न च मे विकारः।
सत्यानृतेति रचना न च मे विकारो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२७।।
verses.english: I am not affected by the illusory world of matter, by the deceit and arrogance or true or false that exist in this creation. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
सन्ध्यादिकालरहितं न च मे वियोगो
ह्यन्तः प्रबोधरहितं बधिरो न मूकः।
एवं विकल्परहितं न च भावशुद्धं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२८।।
verses.english: I am devoid of time, evening or morning. I have no separate parts. I have no interior perception. I am neither mute nor deaf. I do not become pure. I am already pure, free from illusions. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्नाथनाथरहितं हि निराकुलं वै
निश्चित्तचित्तविगतं हि निराकुलं वै।
संविद्धि सर्वविगतं हि निराकुलं वै
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२९।।
verses.english: I am the peaceful one, who is with master and also without him, who is in presence of the mind as well as absence of it. Know well that I am the peaceful one who is beyond everything. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
कान्तारमन्दिरमिदं हि कथं वदामि
संसिद्धसंशयमिदं हि कथं वदामि।
एवं निरन्तरसमं हि निराकुलं वै
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३०।।
verses.english: How can I say this is a wild forest or a temple? How can I say this is proven or doubtful? I am the continuous peaceful presence. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्जीवजीवरहितं सततं विभाति
निर्बीजबीजरहितं सततं विभाति।
निर्वाणबन्धरहितं सततं विभाति
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३१।।
verses.english: The one devoid of life and lifelessness shines forever. The one devoid of source and sourceless-ness shines forever. The one devoid of liberation and bondage shines forever. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
सम्भूतिवर्जितमिदं सततं विभाति
संसारवर्जितमिदं सततं विभाति।
संहारवर्जितमिदं सततं विभाति
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३२।।
verses.english: Devoid of a beginning it shines forever. Devoid of a worldly existence it shines forever. Devoid of destruction it shines forever. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
उल्लेखमात्रमपि ते न च नामरूपं
निर्भिन्नभिन्नमपि ते न हि वस्तु किञ्चित्।
निर्लज्जमानस करोषि कथं विषादं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३३।।
verses.english: Even if it is said, you don't have a name and form. Even if you are differentiated or undifferentiated you are not an object. O shameless mind, why are you suffering? I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न जरा न मृत्युः
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च जन्मदुःखम्।
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते विकारो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३४।।
verses.english: My friend why do you cry, you have no old age, no death, you have no birth, no suffering, you have no defects. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते स्वरूपं
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते विरूपम्।
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते वयांसि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३५।।
verses.english: My friend why do you cry, you have no form, you are not deformed, you are ageless. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like a space.

verses.sanskrit:
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते वयांसि
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते मनांसि।
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न तवेन्द्रियाणि
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३६।।
verses.english: My friend why do you cry, you are not old, you are not a mind, you are not senses. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च तेऽस्ति कामः
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते प्रलोभः।
किं नाम रोदिषि सखे न च ते विमोहो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३७।।
verses.english: My friend why do you cry, you have no lust, you have no greed, you have no attachments. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
ऐश्वर्यमिच्छसि कथं न च ते धनानि
ऐश्वर्यमिच्छसि कथं न च ते हि पत्नी।
ऐश्वर्यमिच्छसि कथं न च ते ममेति
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३८।।
verses.english: Why do you desire for prosperity, you do not have wealth, you do not have a wife, you do not own anything. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
लिङ्गप्रपञ्चजनुषी न च ते न मे च
निर्लज्जमानसमिदं च विभाति भिन्नम्।
निर्भेदभेदरहितं न च ते न मे च
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।३९।।
verses.english: Both you and me are not born of this illusory material world. The shameless mind divides and makes it appear separate. Both you and me are free of division and non-division. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
नो वाणुमात्रमपि ते हि विरागरूपं
नो वाणुमात्रमपि ते हि सरागरूपम्।
नो वाणुमात्रमपि ते हि सकामरूपं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।४०।।
verses.english: Your nature has not even tiniest amount of detachment, not even the tiniest amount of attachment, not even the tiniest amount of desires. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
ध्याता न ते हि हृदये न च ते समाधि
र्ध्यानं न ते हि हृदये न बहिः प्रदेशः।
ध्येयं न चेति हृदये न हि वस्तु कालो
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।४१।।
verses.english: At your core is no absorption, no meditator, at your core is no exterior, no meditation, at your core is no time and space, no object of meditation. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्सारभूतमखिलं कथितं मया ते
न त्वं न मे न महतो न गुरुर्न शिष्यः।
स्वच्छन्दरूपसहजं परमार्थतत्त्वं
ज्ञानामृतं समरसं गगनोपमोऽहम्।।४२।।
verses.english: I have told you the whole essence of everything. You, me, matter, Guru and student do not exist. The ultimate truth is naturally free. I am the essence of all knowledge, omnipresent like space.

verses.sanskrit:
कथमिह परमार्थं तत्त्वमानन्दरूपं
कथमिह परमार्थं नैवमानन्दरूपम्।
कथमिह परमार्थं ज्ञानविज्ञानरूपं
यदि परमहमेकं वर्तते व्योमरूपम्।।४३।।
verses.english: If I am ultimately one, like space, how can the truth be of the nature of bliss, how can the truth be of the nature of suffering, how can the truth be of the nature of knowledge?

verses.sanskrit:
दहनपवनहीनं विद्धि विज्ञानमेक
मवनिजलविहीनं विद्धि विज्ञानरूपम्।
समगमनविहीनं विद्धि विज्ञानमेकं
गगनमिव विशालं विद्धि विज्ञानमेकम्।।४४।।
verses.english: Know that there is only one knowledge - it is not fire, not air, not earth, not water, it never comes and goes, it is like infinite sky.

verses.sanskrit:
न शून्यरूपं न विशून्यरूपं
न शुद्धरूपं न विशुद्धरूपम्।
रूपं विरूपं न भवामि किञ्चित्
स्वरूपरूपं परमार्थतत्त्वम्।।४५।।
verses.english: It is not nothing, not something. It is not pure, not impure. It is not form, not formless. The ultimate truth has its own unique nature.

verses.sanskrit:
मुञ्च मुञ्च हि संसारं त्यागं मुञ्च हि सर्वथा।
त्यागात्यागविषं शुद्धममृतं सहजं ध्रुवम्।।४६।।
verses.english: Renounce the illusory world and completely give up the renouncing. Renunciation as well as clinging, both are poison. Your essence is pure, natural and unchanging.

verses.sanskrit:
नावाहनं नैव विसर्जनं वा
पुष्पाणि पत्राणि कथं भवन्ति।
ध्यानानि मन्त्राणि कथं भवन्ति
समासमं चैव शिवार्चनं च।।१।।
verses.english: What is the use of invocations and offerings, what is the use of flowers and leaves, what is the use of meditation and mantras? I am the same as Consciousness and it's worshipper.

verses.sanskrit:
न केवलं बन्धविबन्धमुक्तो
न केवलं शुद्धविशुद्धमुक्तः।
न केवलं योगवियोगमुक्तः
स वै विमुक्तो गगनोपमोऽहम्।।२।।
verses.english: I am not only free from bondage and unbounded-ness, I am not only free from purity and impurity, I am not only free from union and separation, I am free from everything, I am like space.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जायते सर्वमिदं हि तथ्यं
सञ्जायते सर्वमिदं वितथ्यम्।
एवं विकल्पो मम नैव जातः
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।३।।
verses.english: Everything is the truth or everything is false, such ignorance does not arise in me. I am not the Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
न साञ्जनं चैव निरञ्जनं वा
न चान्तरं वापि निरन्तरं वा।
अन्तर्विभिन्नं न हि मे विभाति
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।४।।
verses.english: I am not perfect and also not imperfect. I am not eternal and also not unceasing. I'm not divided and not undivided. I'm not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
अबोधबोधो मम नैव जातो
बोधस्वरूपं मम नैव जातम्।
निर्बोधबोधं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।५।।
verses.english: I do not have any ignorance or knowledge. Knowledge never arises in me. How can I say about ignorance and knowledge? I'm not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
न धर्मयुक्तो न च पापयुक्तो
न बन्धयुक्तो न च मोक्षयुक्तः।
युक्तं त्वयुक्तं न च मे विभाति
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।६।।
verses.english: I do not contain any right conduct or wrong conduct. I do not contain any bondage and liberation. I do not see contents or absence of contents. I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
परापरं वा न च मे कदाचित्
मध्यस्थभावो हि न चारिमित्रम्।
हिताहितं चापि कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।७।।
verses.english: Within reach and beyond I am not, nor am I in-between. I do not have enemies or friends. How can I say about benefits or harm? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
नोपासको नैवमुपास्यरूपं
न चोपदेशो न च मे क्रिया च।
संवित्स्वरूपं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।८।।
verses.english: I am not the worshiper, I am not the worshipped, I am not the preaching, I am not the practice. What can I say, I am Consciousness. I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
नो व्यापकं व्याप्यमिहास्ति किञ्चित्
न चालयं वापि निरालयं वा।
अशून्यशून्यं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।९।।
verses.english: There is nothing that is pervaded, nothing that pervades. There is no manifestation or dissolution. What can I say about existence or non-existence? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
न ग्राहको ग्राह्यकमेव किञ्चित्
न कारणं वा मम नैव कार्यम्।
अचिन्त्यचिन्त्यं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१०।।
verses.english: I am not the perceiver, not the perceived. I am not the cause, not the effect. What can I say about thinkable and unthinkable? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
न भेदकं वापि न चैव भेद्यं
न वेदकं वा मम नैव वेद्यम्।
गतागतं तात कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।११।।
verses.english: There is no discrimination, no discriminated, no knower and no known. What can I say about coming and going? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
न चास्ति देहो न च मे विदेहो
बुद्धिर्मनो मे न हि चेन्द्रियाणि।
रागो विरागश्च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१२।।
verses.english: I am not the body, I am not disembodied. I do not have a mind, intellect or senses. What can I say about attachment and detachment? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
उल्लेखमात्रं न हि भिन्नमुच्चै
रुल्लेखमात्रं न तिरोहितं वै।
समासमं मित्र कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१३।।
verses.english: One cannot mention that, which is divided. One cannot mention that, which never appeared. What can I say, my friend, about differences and similarities? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
जितेन्द्रियोऽहं त्वजितेन्द्रियो वा
न संयमो मे नियमो न जातः।
जयाजयौ मित्र कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१४।।
verses.english: I have never conquered the senses, I do not know restraint and rules. What can I say about victory and defeat, my friend? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
अमूर्तमूर्तिर्न च मे कदाचिदा
द्यन्तमध्यं न च मे कदाचित्।
बलाबलं मित्र कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१५।।
verses.english: I am not form or formlessness. I have no start, no middle and no end. What can I say about strength and weakness? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
मृतामृतं वापि विषाविषं च
सञ्जायते तात न मे कदाचित्।
अशुद्धशुद्धं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१६।।
verses.english: I do not die, I am not immortal. I do not have poison nor am I non-poisonous. These, my son, I do not know. What can I say about pure and impure? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वप्नः प्रबोधो न च योगमुद्रा
नक्तं दिवा वापि न मे कदाचित्।
अतुर्यतुर्यं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१७।।
verses.english: I do not dream, I do not sleep, I do not wake up, I am not in any yogic state. I do not know day or night. What can I say about transcendental or non-transcendental? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
संविद्धि मां सर्वविसर्वमुक्तं
माया विमाया न च मे कदाचित्।
सन्ध्यादिकं कर्म कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१८।।
verses.english: Know me well as free from everything and nothing. I do not have illusions or reality. What can I say about morning and evening rituals? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
संविद्धि मां सर्वसमाधियुक्तं
संविद्धि मां लक्ष्यविलक्ष्यमुक्तम्।
योगं वियोगं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।१९।।
verses.english: Know me well as always in Samadhi, know me well as free from goals and non-goals. What can I say about union and separation? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
मूर्खोऽपि नाहं न च पण्डितोऽहं
मौनं विमौनं न च मे कदाचित्।
तर्कं वितर्कं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।२०।।
verses.english: I am not an idiot, I am not a wise. I do not observe silence or non-silence. What can I say about logic and argument? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
पिता च माता च कुलं न जातिर्
जन्मादि मृत्युर्न च मे कदाचित्।
स्नेहं विमोहं च कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।२१।।
verses.english: I never had a father, mother, family, caste, never born, never die. What can I say of affection and attachments? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
अस्तं गतो नैव सदोदितोऽहं
तेजोवितेजो न च मे कदाचित्।
सन्ध्यादिकं कर्म कथं वदामि
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।२२।।
verses.english: I do not come and go. I am always manifested. I do not have light and darkness. What can I say about morning and evening rituals? I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
असंशयं विद्धि नराकुलं माम
संशयं विद्धि निरन्तरं माम्।
असंशयं विद्धि निरञ्जनं मां
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।२३।।
verses.english: Know without doubt, I am without suffering, without break, without any imperfection. I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
ध्यानानि सर्वाणि परित्यजन्ति
शुभाशुभं कर्म परित्यजन्ति।
त्यागामृतं तात पिबन्ति धीराः
स्वरूपनिर्वाणमनामयोऽहम्।।२४।।
verses.english: I have given up meditation. I have given up good and bad actions. I am drinking the nectar of renunciation. I am not Maya, my nature is freedom.

verses.sanskrit:
विन्दति विन्दति न हि न हि यत्र
छन्दोलक्षणं न हि न हि यत्र।
समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः
प्रलपति तत्त्वं परमवधूतः।।२५।।
verses.english: Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.

verses.sanskrit:
ॐ इति गदितं गगनसमं तत्
न परापरसारविचार इति ।
अविलासविलासनिराकरणं
कथमक्षरबिन्दुसमुच्चरणम् ।। १।।
verses.english: As it is chanted, the Aum is like space. It has no high or low. The play of light and dark is never obstructed. How can the dot on Aum have any effect?

verses.sanskrit:
इति तत्त्वमसिप्रभृतिश्रुतिभिः
प्रतिपादितमात्मनि तत्त्वमसि।
त्वमुपाधिविवर्जितसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२।।
verses.english: ”You are that” - the scriptures say. “You are that” -  the Consciousness proves. You are beyond any titles. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
अधऊध्र्वविवर्जितसर्वसमं
बहिरन्तरवर्जितसर्वसमम्।
यदि चैकविवर्जितसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।३।।
verses.english: Beyond up and down, beyond interior and exterior, if there is only the one and the same Consciousness in all, why are you crying O mind I am the same Consciousness in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कल्पितकल्पविचार इति
न हि कारणकार्यविचार इति।
पदसन्धिविवर्जितसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।४।।
verses.english: There is no discrimination between imagined and imagination. There is no discrimination between the cause and effect. There is no discrimination between poem and it's words. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि बोधविबोधसमाधिरिति
न हि देशविदेशसमाधिरिति।
न हि कालविकालसमाधिरिति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।५।।
verses.english: There is no knowledge and no ignorance in the one. There is no space or lack of space in the one. There is no time and eternity in the one. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कुम्भनभो न हि कुम्भ इति
न हि जीववपुर्न हि जीव इति।
न हि कारणकार्यविभाग इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।६।।
verses.english: There is no space in the pot. There is no soul, there are no beings with soul. There is no causal body or any actions of it. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह सर्वनिरन्तरमोक्षपदं
लघुदीर्घविचारविहीन इति।
न हि वर्तुलकोणविभाग इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।७।।
verses.english: It is ever free, undifferentiated all. It is not big or small. It is not circular or angular. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह शून्यविशून्यविहीन इति
इह शुद्धविशुद्धविहीन इति।
इह सर्वविसर्वविहीन इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।८।।
verses.english: It is free of something or nothing. It is free of pure and impure. It is free of everything. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि भिन्नविभिन्नविचार इति
बहिरन्तरसन्धिविचार इति।
अरिमित्रविवर्जितसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।९।।
verses.english: There is no distinction of difference or non-difference. There is no distinction of interior or exterior. There is no distinction between friend and enemy in it. It is all one equally. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि शिष्यविशिष्यस्वरूप इति
न चराचरभेदविचार इति।
इह सर्वनिरन्तरमोक्षपदं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१०।।
verses.english: It is not in the form of student or non-student. It is not differentiated into living or inert. It is everything, eternally free. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
ननु रूपविरूपविहीन इति
ननु भिन्नविभिन्नविहीन इति।
ननु सर्गविसर्गविहीन इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।११।।
verses.english: It has no form and formlessness. It has no division or divisionless-ness. It does not have creation or destruction. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न गुणागुणपाशनिबन्ध इति
मृतजीवनकर्म करोमि कथम्।
इति शुद्धनिरञ्जनसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१२।।
verses.english: I am not bound by any qualities or non-qualities. How can I do the actions of life and death. I am pure, spotless, same in everything. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह भावविभावविहीन इति
इह कामविकामविहीन इति।
इह बोधतमं खलु मोक्षसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१३।।
verses.english: Here there is no existence or non-existence. Here there is no desire or desireless-ness. I know that here there is nothing but freedom. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह तत्त्वनिरन्तरतत्त्वमिति
न हि सन्धिविसन्धिविहीन इति।
यदि सर्वविवर्जितसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१४।।
verses.english: Here is the truth which is undifferentiated. It does not have joints or disjoints. If it is free from everything and same in everything why do you cry O mind? I am the same in everything. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
अनिकेतकुटी परिवारसमं
इह सङ्गविसङ्गविहीनपरम्।
इह बोधविबोधविहीनपरं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१५।।
verses.english: I do not dwell in a house, do not have a family. Here there is no association or disassociation, there is no knowledge or ignorance ultimately. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
अविकारविकारमसत्यमिति
अविलक्षविलक्षमसत्यमिति।
यदि केवलमात्मनि सत्यमिति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१६।।
verses.english: Changing or unchanging, is not the truth. Purposeful or purposeless, is not the truth. If the only truth is Consciousness, why are you crying O mind? I am in same in everything. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह सर्वसमं खलु जीव इति
इह सर्वनिरन्तरजीव इति।
इह केवलनिश्चलजीव इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१७।।
verses.english: Here everything equally is life. Certainly here everything is life which is undifferentiated. Here there is only life which is pure. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
अविवेकविवेकमबोध इति
अविकल्पविकल्पमबोध इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरबोध इति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१८।।
verses.english: It is known as both discrimination and indiscrimination. It is known as both ignorance and knowledge. If there is this undifferentiated knowledge, why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मोक्षपदं न हि बन्धपदं
न हि पुण्यपदं न हि पापपदम्।
न हि पूर्णपदं न हि रिक्तपदं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।१९।।
verses.english: There is no state of liberation or bondage. There is no state of right conduct or wrong conduct. There is no state of perfection or imperfection. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि वर्णविवर्णविहीनसमं
यदि कारणकार्यविहीनसमम्।
यदि भेदविभेदविहीनसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२०।।
verses.english: If I am equal, free of caste and casteless-ness, if I am equal, free of causes and effects, if I am equal, free of division and non-division, why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह सर्वनिरन्तरसर्वचिते
इह केवलनिश्चलसर्वचिते।
द्विपदादिविवर्जितसर्वचिते
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२१।।
verses.english: Here all minds are one unbroken and undivided. Here all minds are only pure. Here there are no men, everything is mind. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
अतिसर्वनिरन्तरसर्वगतं
अतिनिर्मलनिश्चलसर्वगतम्।
दिनरात्रिविवर्जितसर्वगतं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२२।।
verses.english: It is above all, undifferentiated, omnipresent. It is purest and innocent omnipresence. It is free from day or night. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि बन्धविबन्धसमागमनं
न हि योगवियोगसमागमनम्।
न हि तर्कवितर्कसमागमनं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२३।।
verses.english: It is not a meeting of freedom and bondage. It is not a meeting of union and separation. It is not a meeting of logic and arguments. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह कालविकालनिराकरणं
अणुमात्रकृशानुनिराकरणम्।
न हि केवलसत्यनिराकरणं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२४।।
verses.english: Here time and timelessness do not exist. Here atoms and molecules do not exist. Here only the ultimate truth exists. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह देहविदेहविहीन इति
ननु स्वप्नसुषुप्तिविहीनपरम्।
अभिधानविधानविहीनपरं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२५।।
verses.english: Here there is no body or disembodiment. Nor is there dreaming or deep sleep. This ultimate has no names and titles. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
गगनोपमशुद्धविशालसमं
अतिसर्वविवर्जितसर्वसमम्।
गतसारविसारविकारसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२६।।
verses.english: Just like the metaphorical space, it is omnipresent, pure and immense. It has presence and absence of everything. It has everything essential and nonessential. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह धर्मविधर्मविरागतर
मिह वस्तुविवस्तुविरागतरम्।
इह कामविकामविरागतरं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२७।।
verses.english: Here there is detachment from right conduct and wrong conduct. Here there is a detachment from objects and non-objects. Here there is detachment from desire and desireless-ness. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
सुखदुःखविवर्जितसर्वसम
मिह शोकविशोकविहीनपरम्।
गुरुशिष्यविवर्जिततत्त्वपरं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२८।।
verses.english: It is everything equally, free from happiness and suffering. It is the ultimate, free from pleasures and pains. It is the ultimate, has no Guru and student. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
न किलाङ्कुरसारविसार इति
न चलाचलसाम्यविसाम्यमिति।
अविचारविचारविहीनमिति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।२९।।
verses.english: It is certainly not a seed. It is not essence and details. It is not living and dead. It is not similar or dissimilar. It is not introspection or absence of thought. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
इह सारसमुच्चयसारमिति
कथितं निजभावविभेद इति।
विषये करणत्वमसत्यमिति
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।३०।।
verses.english: It is known as the essence of all essences. How can you find differences in one's own existence? It is not true that you are the perceiver of objects. Why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
बहुधा श्रुतयः प्रवदन्ति यतो
वियदादिरिदं मृगतोयसमम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वसमं
किमु रोदिषि मानसि सर्वसमम्।।३१।।
verses.english: As the scriptures say - the existence is like a mirage. If it is the same everywhere and eternal, why do you cry O mind? You are the same in everything.

verses.sanskrit:
विन्दति विन्दति न हि न हि यत्र
छन्दोलक्षणं न हि न हि तत्र।
समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः
प्रलपति तत्त्वं परमवधूतः।।३२।।
verses.english: Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.

verses.sanskrit:
बहुधा श्रुतयः प्रवदन्ति वयं
वियदादिरिदं मृगतोयसमम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव
मुपमेयमथोह्युपमा च कथम्।।१।।
verses.english: As the scriptures say in many ways, the manifested universe is like a mirage. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be comparison and compared?

verses.sanskrit:
अविभक्तिविभक्तिविहीनपरं
ननु कार्यविकार्यविहीनपरम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
यजनं च कथं तपनं च कथम्।।२।।
verses.english: Ultimately it is free from divisions and non-divisions, ultimately it is free from activity and rest. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be worship and practice?

verses.sanskrit:
मन एव निरन्तरसर्वगतं
ह्यविशालविशालविहीनपरम्।
मन एव निरन्तरसर्वशिवं
मनसापि कथं वचसा च कथम्।।३।।
verses.english: It is the mind that is eternally omnipresent. Ultimately it is not big not small. Mind is nothing but eternal Consciousness. How can it be expressed via thought and speech?

verses.sanskrit:
दिनरात्रिविभेदनिराकरण
मुदितानुदितस्य निराकरणम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
रविचन्द्रमसौ ज्वलनश्च कथम्।।४।।
verses.english: It is without the distinction of day and night. It is without the distinction of rising and setting. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be Sun, moon and fire?

verses.sanskrit:
गतकामविकामविभेद इति
गतचेष्टविचेष्टविभेद इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
बहिरन्तरभिन्नमतिश्च कथम्।।५।।
verses.english: It is beyond the difference of desire and satisfaction. It is beyond the difference of effort and non-effort. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be differences of internal or external?

verses.sanskrit:
यदि सारविसारविहीन इति
यदि शून्यविशून्यविहीन इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
प्रथमं च कथं चरमं च कथम्।।६।।
verses.english: If it is devoid of essence and details, if it is devoid of nothing and something, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be a beginning and an end?

verses.sanskrit:
यदि भेदविभेदनिराकरणं
यदि वेदकवेद्यनिराकरणम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
तृतीयं च कथं तुरीयं च कथम्।।७।।
verses.english: If there is no distinction of difference and similarity, if there is no distinction of knower and the known, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be the third or the forth?

verses.sanskrit:
गदितागदितं न हि सत्यमिति
विदिताविदितं न हि सत्यमिति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
विषयेन्द्रियबुद्धिमनांसि कथम्।।८।।
verses.english: The chanted one and the untold are not the truth. The known and unknown are not the truth. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be objects, senses, intellect and mind?

verses.sanskrit:
गगनं पवनो न हि सत्यमिति
धरणी दहनो न हि सत्यमिति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
जलदश्च कथं सलिलं च कथम्।।९।।
verses.english: Sky and air are not the truth. Earth and fire are not the truth. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be clouds or water?

verses.sanskrit:
यदि कल्पितलोकनिराकरणं
यदि कल्पितदेवनिराकरणम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
गुणदोषविचारमतिश्च कथम्।।१०।।
verses.english: If there is no distinction between imagination and the world. If there is no distinction between imagination and the gods. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination of qualities and defects?

verses.sanskrit:
मरणामरणं हि निराकरणं
करणाकरणं हि निराकरणम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
गमनागमनं हि कथं वदति।।११।।
verses.english: There is no distinction between life and death. There is no distinction between action and inaction. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be transmigration?

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतिः पुरुषो न हि भेद इति
न हि कारणकार्यविभेद इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
पुरुषापुरुषं च कथं वदति।।१२।।
verses.english: There is no difference between Purusha and the Prakriti. There is no difference between cause and effect. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can I say about Purusha and not-Purusha?

verses.sanskrit:
तृतीयां न हि दुःखसमागमनं
न गुणाद्द्वितीयस्य समागमनम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
स्थविरश्च युवा च शिशुश्च कथम्।।१३।।
verses.english: Suffering of the third kind does not arise. Quality of the second kind does not arise. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be old age, youth and infancy?

verses.sanskrit:
ननु आश्रमवर्णविहीनपरं
ननु कारणकर्तृविहीनपरम्।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव
मविनष्टविनष्टमतिश्च कथम्।।१४।।
verses.english: In it there is no castes or stages of life. In it there is no cause or doer ultimately. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination between destructible and indestructible?

verses.sanskrit:
ग्रसिताग्रसितं च वितथ्यमिति
जनिताजनितं च वितथ्यमिति।
यदि चैकनिन्तरसर्वशिव
मविनाशि विनाशि कथं हि भवेत्।।१५।।
verses.english: Swallowed and un-swallowed are both false. Born and unborn are both false. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, then how can there be destruction and creation?

verses.sanskrit:
पुरुषापुरुषस्य विनष्टमिति
वनितावनितस्य विनष्टमिति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव
मविनोदविनोदमतिश्च कथम्।।१६।।
verses.english: Masculine and non-masculine are not present in it. Feminine and non-feminine are not present in it. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination of joy and sorrow?

verses.sanskrit:
यदि मोहविषादविहीनपरो
यदि संशयशोकविहीनपरः।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव
महमेति ममेति कथं च पुनः।।१७।।
verses.english: If it is ultimately devoid of attachment and misery, if it is ultimately devoid of doubt and grief, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be I and mine?

verses.sanskrit:
ननु धर्मविधर्मविनाश इति
ननु बन्धविबन्धविनाश इति।
यदि चैकनिन्तरसर्वशिव
मिहदुःखविदुःखमतिश्च कथम्।।१८।।
verses.english: It is not a destroyer of right conduct or wrong conduct. It is not a destroyer of bondage and non bondage. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be suffering or happiness?

verses.sanskrit:
हि याज्ञिकयज्ञविभाग इति
न हुताशनवस्तुविभाग इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
वद कर्मफलानि भवन्ति कथम्।।१९।।
verses.english: There is no distinction of rituals of sacrifice and conductors of rituals. There is no distinction of sacrificial fire and offerings into the fire. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be consequences of actions?

verses.sanskrit:
ननु शोकविशोकविमुक्त इति
ननु दर्पविदर्पविमुक्त इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
ननु रागविरागमतिश्च कथम्।।२०।।
verses.english: It is free of sadness and joy it is free of pride and humility. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be attachments and detachments?

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मोहविमोहविकार इति
न हि लोभविलोभविकार इति।
यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं
ह्यविवेकविवेकमतिश्च कथम्।।२१।।
verses.english: Detachments and attachments do not arise in it. Greed and contentment do not arise in it. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination and indiscrimination?

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमहं न हि हन्त कदाचिदपि
कुलजातिविचारमसत्यमिति।
अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति
अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२२।।
verses.english: You and I were never existed. Family and caste are not true. It is the ultimate truth that I am Consciousness. Whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit:
गुरुशिष्यविचारविशीर्ण इति
उपदेशविचारविशीर्ण इति।
अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति
अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२३।।
verses.english: In it, the distinction of teacher and student vanishes. The teaching also vanishes. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कल्पितदेहविभाग इति
न हि कल्पितलोकविभाग इति।
अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति
अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२४।।
verses.english: There is no difference between imagination and bodies. There is no difference between imagination and worlds. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit:
सरजो विरजो न कदाचिदपि
ननु निर्मलनिश्चलशुद्ध इति।
अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति
अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२५।।
verses.english: I never was with action and without action. I am pure innocent and spotless. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit:
न हि देहविदेहविकल्प इति
अनृतं चरितं न हि सत्यमिति।
अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति
अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२६।।
verses.english: Distinction of embodied and disembodied is not there. Immoral and moral are not true. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit:
विन्दति विन्दति न हि न हि यत्र
छन्दोलक्षणं न हि न हि तत्र।
समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः
प्रलपति तत्त्वं परमवधूतः।।२७।।
verses.english: Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.

verses.sanskrit:
रथ्याकर्पटविरचितकन्थः
पुण्यापुण्यविवर्जितपन्थः।
शून्यागारे तिष्ठति नग्नो
शुद्धनिरञ्जनसमरसमग्नः।।१।।
verses.english: Clad in rejected clothes thrown on the streets, he follows a path free of right or wrong actions. He sits naked in an empty dwelling, absorbed in the pure stainless eternal one.

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्यालक्ष्यविवर्जितलक्ष्यो
युक्तायुक्तविवर्जितदक्षः।
केवलतत्त्वनिरञ्जनपूतो
वादविवादः कथमवधूतः।।२।।
verses.english: Having a goal devoid of goals, with a skill devoid of skills, he knows only pure truth. How can an Avadhuta engage in arguments?

verses.sanskrit:
आशापाशविबन्धनमुक्ताः
शौचाचारविवर्जितयुक्ताः।
एवं सर्वविवर्जितसन्त
स्तत्त्वं शुद्धनिरञ्जनवन्तः।।३।।
verses.english: Free from the chains of hope. Devoid of manners and purification. Renouncing everything, he becomes peaceful. He becomes the truth - pure and stainless.

verses.sanskrit:
कथमिह देहविदेहविचारः
कथमिह रागविरागविचारः।
निर्मलनिश्चलगगनाकारं
स्वयमिह तत्त्वं सहजाकारम्।।४।।
verses.english: How can there be questions of embodiment or disembodiment? How can there be questions of attachment and detachment? He is like sky - pure and innocent. He himself is the natural truth.

verses.sanskrit:
कथमिह तत्त्वं विन्दति यत्र
रूपमरूपं कथमिह तत्र।
गगनाकारः परमो यत्र
विषयीकरणं कथमिह तत्र।।५।।
verses.english: Where the truth is known, how can there be forms and formlessness. If the ultimate is like space, how can there be objectification?

verses.sanskrit:
गगनाकारनिरन्तरहंस
स्तत्त्वविशुद्धनिरञ्जनहंसः।
एवं कथमिह भिन्नविभिन्नं
बन्धविबन्धविकारविभिन्नम्।।६।।
verses.english: The swan is eternal like space. The swan is clear and spotless truth. How can there be differences or liberation and divisions?

verses.sanskrit:
केवलतत्त्वनिरन्तरसर्वं
योगवियोगौ कथमिह गर्वम्।
एवं परमनिरन्तरसर्व
मेवं कथमिह सारविसारम्।।७।।
verses.english: Everything is one eternal truth. How can there be union or separation or the pride that arises from that. In that everything is ultimately eternal. How can there be any substance or non-substance?

verses.sanskrit:
केवलतत्त्वनिरञ्जनसर्वं
गगनाकारनिरन्तरशुद्धम्।
एवं कथमिह सङ्गविसङ्गं
सत्यं कथमिह रङ्गविरङ्गम्।।८।।
verses.english: Everything is one eternal truth. It is like space pure and eternal. How can there be association and dissociation? How can the relations and non-relations be the truth?

verses.sanskrit:
योगवियोगै रहितो योगी
भोगविभोगै रहितो भोगी।
एवं चरति हि मन्दं मन्दं
मनसा कल्पितसहजानन्दम्।।९।।
verses.english: The Yogi is devoid of union or separation. The enjoyer is devoid of enjoyment or lack of it. He wanders leisurely. His mind is filled with natural bliss.

verses.sanskrit:
बोधविबोधैः सततं युक्तो
द्वैताद्वैतैः कथमिह मुक्तः
सहजो विरजः कथमिह योगी
शुद्धनिरञ्जनसमरसभोगी।।१०।।
verses.english: One who is always in knowledge and ignorance, how can he be free from duality and nonduality? How is the Yogi naturally detached?  - By becoming the enjoyer of pure stainless eternal bliss.

verses.sanskrit:
भग्नाभग्नविवर्जितभग्नो
लग्नालग्नविवर्जितलग्नः।
एवं कथमिह सारविसारः
समरसतत्त्वं गगनाकारः।।११।।
